name: Vesak
traditions: Visiting temples; meditation and prayer; bathing Buddha statues; releasing caged birds; lantern processions; acts of generosity to monks and the poor.
historicalBackground: Vesak celebrates the birth, enlightenment, and death of Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha). It is the most sacred day in the Buddhist calendar, observed since ancient times.
description: The most important Buddhist festival, celebrating the birth, enlightenment, and passing of the Buddha on the full moon day of May.
